capabilities_used: ls introspection of topics/ and sub_*.fifo
what_worked_well: Zero-config start did exactly what the persona wants: 'bash src/shellmux serve $D --unix $SOCK &' came up with no config file, no port registry, no service unit. Matches DEMO.md Beat 2 verbatim.

Topic-scoped fan-out is correct and intact. Two weather subscribers each received 'storm-incoming-42' byte-for-byte; the sports subscriber received exactly 0 bytes. Re-ran with three ordered messages (alpha/bravo/charlie) x5 trials: both weather subs got [alpha,bravo,charlie] in order every time, sports stayed empty every time.
Truly silent on the happy path. Audited broker.log, every subscriber's stderr, and pub stdout+stderr: total_err_bytes=0 across all 5 reproducibility runs. No spurious warnings, no noise.

Introspection via plain ls works as advertised: 'ls $D/topics/' showed {sports, weather}; 'ls $D/topics/weather/sub_*.fifo | wc -l' = 2, sports = 1. No bespoke admin command needed (persona explicitly values this).

Forget-on-death is clean: killing one of two weather subs dropped the FIFO count 2->1 within ~2s, and a subsequent publish reached the survivor ('after-death') with pub stderr = 0 bytes — the dead sub's stranded FIFO caused no error for the publisher.
wished_for: A one-line 'hello world' fan-out example near the TOP of README.md. The README and DEMO.md both lead with the deadline-scheduler chaos proof (the contribution), which is correct for a judge — but a homelab operator skims for the dumbest possible pub/sub snippet first. DEMO.md Beat 2 has it, but it's mixed into a --delay example; a bare serve/sub/pub triad would shave a minute off first use.

Consistent subcommand casing in prose. The CLAUDE.md/spec mission paragraphs say 'SUB'/'PUB' (uppercase) while the actual CLI verbs are lowercase 'sub'/'pub' (as shown in DEMO.md and confirmed by my runs). The runnable docs are correct; only the conceptual prose uses uppercase. A copy-paste-from-the-mission-text user would hit a momentary 'why doesn't SUB work' before noticing the lowercase in DEMO.md.
evidence_log: Image present: 'docker images | grep shellmux' -> shellmux-dev:latest 159MB
Scenario script run: docker run --rm -v $PWD:/work -w /work shellmux-dev bash /work/.eval-scratch/broker-operator-fanout.sh
Topics after subscribe: 'ls $D/topics/' -> sports / weather ; weather sub fifos: 2 ; sports sub fifos: 1
weather sub 1 (w1.out): 'storm-incoming-42' ; weather sub 2 (w2.out): 'storm-incoming-42' ; sports sub 1 (s1.out): <empty>
Error audit: broker.log bytes=0, w1.err=0, w2.err=0, s1.err=0, pub.out=0, pub.err=0
Reproducibility (5 runs, 3 ordered msgs each): run#1..#5 all w1=[alpha,bravo,charlie,] w2=[alpha,bravo,charlie,] sports_bytes=0 total_err_bytes=0
forget-on-death: before kill weather fifos=2 ; after killing one sub weather fifos=1 ; survivor got [after-death,] ; pub2 err bytes: 0

friction_points.description: The first-read docs (README.md, DEMO.md) lead with the deadline-scheduler chaos proof and the simplest serve/sub/pub fan-out example is only shown inside a --delay example (DEMO.md Beat 2, lines 50-57). A homelab operator just wants the bare three-line pub/sub triad up top to confirm the basic thing works before reading the contribution pitch. Mild discovery friction only; the actual behavior is flawless.
friction_points.evidence: README.md lines 1-9 and DEMO.md lines 23-46 are the chaos proof; the bare fan-out (serve/sub/pub) is mixed into the '--delay 5' example at DEMO.md:50-57. No standalone 'two subs, one publish' example exists in the user-facing docs.
friction_points.workaround: I derived the fan-out invocation from the evaluator recipe + DEMO.md Beat 2 and it worked first try; an operator would do the same in ~1 minute.

friction_points.description: Conceptual prose (CLAUDE.md/spec mission) refers to the verbs as 'SUB'/'PUB' uppercase, while the actual CLI verbs are lowercase 'sub'/'pub'. Persona explicitly cares about clarity of how to subscribe vs publish; an operator copying the uppercase token from the mission text would hit a brief stumble.
friction_points.evidence: CLAUDE.md mission/DoD prose uses SUB/PUB uppercase; DEMO.md:53-54 and my working runs use lowercase 'sub'/'pub'. Runnable docs (DEMO.md) are correct.
friction_points.workaround: Use lowercase as shown in DEMO.md and the evaluator recipe.

free_text: CHALLENGE PASSED — completed:true, verified from real container runs (not imagined). The exact-fan-out requirement is met cleanly and reproducibly: two subscribers on topic 'weather' both received the single published message byte-for-byte ('storm-incoming-42'), the off-topic 'sports' subscriber received exactly 0 bytes, and there was zero error output anywhere (broker log, all three subscribers' stderr, and the publisher's stdout+stderr were all 0 bytes). I re-ran the scenario 5x with 3 ordered messages each to rule out lucky timing — every run delivered [alpha,bravo,charlie] in order to both weather subs and nothing to sports, total_err_bytes=0 across the board. As a homelab operator I also checked the two things I always check beyond the literal ask: introspection ('ls $D/topics/' and 'ls .../sub_*.fifo | wc -l' showed the live subscriber counts with no special admin CLI — exactly what I want) and forget-on-death (killing one weather sub dropped its FIFO 2->1 within ~2s and a later publish still reached the survivor without erroring on the dead sub's stranded pipe — the stranded-FIFO bad-state-is-impossible claim from DEMO.md held). Zero-config start matched the persona's top priority: a single 'serve' line with no config/port registry/service unit. Note: this challenge does NOT touch the deadline proof (chaos/delay/crash), so the negative-control scrutiny rule didn't apply here; I evaluated fan-out, isolation, and silence only, which the docs themselves correctly label as the FREE-from-fork parts, not the contribution. Only friction is cosmetic doc discoverability (no bare hello-world fan-out at the top; SUB/PUB casing in prose vs lowercase CLI) — both low severity, neither blocked me. Strong showing; I'd run this on my homelab box.

proof_assessment.reason: This challenge is the FREE-from-fork fan-out/isolation path, not the deadline-scheduler contribution. The missed=0/dup=0 + must-fail-negative-control proof spine was not exercised here and is out of scope for prod-pub-sub-fanout. No proof claims were made or tested in this session.
proof_assessment.fanout_correctness_observed: exactly-2-of-3 subscribers received the message, off-topic received nothing, 5/5 reproducible, 0 error bytes

claim_audit.claim: README.md:33-34 — 'a homelab event bus' is a target use case; fan-out reaches every live subscriber and nobody else
claim_audit.evidence: 2 weather subs both received 'storm-incoming-42'; sports sub received 0 bytes; reproduced 5x

claim_audit.claim: DEMO.md:108-109 — stranded-FIFO bad state is made impossible (write to vanished pipe is harmless)
claim_audit.evidence: killed one weather sub; subsequent publish reached survivor with pub stderr=0 bytes, no error on the dead sub's FIFO

claim_audit.claim: DEMO.md:79-81 / README 'forget-on-death come free from fork' — subscriber count visible via ls and pruned on death
claim_audit.evidence: ls .../sub_*.fifo|wc -l = 2 before, = 1 within ~2s after killing one sub

claim_audit.claim: persona expectation: zero-config start, no config/port registry/service unit
claim_audit.evidence: single 'serve $D --unix $SOCK &' line brought the broker up; broker.log 0 bytes
